title: Tanarius helferi
intro: Tanarius helferi, also known as Tanarius or Tanacetum, is a flowering plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Tanarius helferi include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and terpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Tanarius helferi include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory conditions.
taxonomy: Tanarius helferi is classified within the family Lamiaceae. It belongs to the genus Tanarius, which is a genus of flowering plants. The species T. helferi is native to Southeast Asia and is known for its unique appearance and growth habits. Tanarius helferi is a relatively new addition to the botanical world, and more research is being conducted to fully understand its classification and characteristics. As a result, the classification of Tanarius helferi is still evolving and may be subject to change as new information becomes available.
uses: Tanarius helferi is a species of aquatic plant, but limited information is available regarding its medicinal uses. side_effects:
I couldn't find any information about the plant Tanarius helferi. 

However, if you are referring to the plant Tanichthys albonubes but commonly known as Helfer's rasboras, then Tanarius helferi is actually another name for Helfer's rasboras, a species of freshwater fish. 

Tanarius helferi can be prone to disease if not properly cared for in aquariums, and improper water conditions can cause stress to the fish leading to secondary infections. 

When stressed or diseased, the fish may exhibit side effects such as labored breathing, lethargy, and discoloration of the scales or fins. 

The side effects can be treated with proper aquarium management and the use of antibiotics or other fish medications if necessary.
